indication: Atrial fibrillation, hypertension, diabetes mellitus, baseline assessment of carotid atherosclerosis for stroke risk stratification
technique: Gray-scale, color flow, and spectral Doppler evaluation of the extracranial carotid arteries and vertebral arteries was performed bilaterally.

rightCarotid.cca: Normal caliber and flow. IMT 0.9 mm.
rightCarotid.ica: Mild heterogeneous plaque at the origin extending into the proximal ICA. Peak systolic velocity 92 cm/s. End diastolic velocity 30 cm/s. ICA/CCA ratio 1.3. Estimated stenosis <50%.

leftCarotid.cca: Normal caliber and flow. IMT 0.8 mm.
leftCarotid.ica: Minimal calcified plaque at the bulb. Peak systolic velocity 80 cm/s. End diastolic velocity 26 cm/s. ICA/CCA ratio 1.2. Estimated stenosis <50%.

findings.vertebralArteries: Bilateral vertebral arteries demonstrate antegrade flow. Normal caliber.

impression: Bilateral mild carotid atherosclerosis with estimated less than 50% stenosis of both internal carotid arteries.
Mildly increased intimal-medial thickness bilaterally, consistent with generalized atherosclerotic disease in the setting of diabetes and hypertension.
Recommend follow-up carotid ultrasound in 1-2 years.
comparison: No prior carotid ultrasound available for comparison.
